aliases: Girl Scouts of the United States of America
description: non-profit organization in the USA
description_wiki: Girl Scouts of the United States of America (GSUSA), commonly referred to as Girl Scouts, is a youth organization for girls in the United States and American girls living abroad.

description: teaching method
description_wiki: The Suzuki method is a mid-20th-century music curriculum and teaching method created by Japanese violinist and pedagogue Shinichi Suzuki.

description: American folk singer (1919–2014)
description_wiki: Peter Seeger (May 3, 1919 – January 27, 2014) was an American singer, songwriter, musician, and left-wing social activist.

description: folk music singer
description_wiki: John McCutcheon (born August 14, 1952) is an American folk music singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist who has produced 45 albums since the 1970s.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: mountain range in western North America
description_wiki: The Cascade Range, or Cascades, is a major mountain range of western North America, extending from southern British Columbia through Washington and Oregon to Northern California.

description: city in and county seat of Thurston County, Washington, United States, and Washington federated state capital city
description_wiki: Olympia (Lushootseed: st̕əč̓as) is the capital city of the U.S. state of Washington.

description: religious nonviolent organization in the United Kingdom
description_wiki: The Fellowship of Reconciliation (FoR or FOR) is the name used by a number of religious nonviolent organizations, particularly in English-speaking countries.

description: Ghost town in Kitsap County, Washington, USA
description_wiki: Seabeck is an unincorporated community and census-designated place (CDP) in Kitsap County, Washington, United States.

description_wiki: Service Employees International Union (SEIU) is a labor union representing 2 million workers in over 100 occupations in the United States, Canada, and Puerto Rico.

description: hospital in Washington, United States
description_wiki: Harborview Medical Center is a public hospital located in the First Hill neighborhood of Seattle, Washington, United States.

description: highway in California, Oregon, and Washington in the United States
description_wiki: Interstate 5 (I-5) is the main north–south Interstate Highway on the West Coast of the United States, running largely parallel to the Pacific coast of the contiguous U.S. from Mexico to Canada.

description: Swedish-American labor activist, songwriter, and member of the Industrial Workers of the World
description_wiki: Joe Hill (born Joel Emmanuel Hägglund; October 7, 1879 – November 19, 1915) was a Swedish-American labor activist, songwriter, and member of the Industrial Workers of the World (IWW, familiarly called the "Wobblies").
